compiled_state.title: Write structural boundary tests for Part C relates_to link suggestion
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description

Write structural boundary tests that verify the epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md contains the required relates_to link suggestion behavior after Part C scan. Per the behavioral testing standard, instruction files are tested at the structural boundary (not content).

### Test Scenarios
1. **Part C section contains relates_to link suggestion step** — verify the keyword/section exists
2. **User approval gate present** — verify the instruction includes user approval before link creation
3. **Open-only filter documented** — verify instructions specify filtering by status=open
4. **Link creation via ticket CLI** — verify instructions reference the ticket link command for relates_to

### File Impact
- tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline-relates-to.sh (create — new test file)

### Implementation Notes
- Use grep-based structural assertions against the skill file
- Test that the expected sections/keywords are present
- Do NOT test the content of instructions (test the boundary, not the content)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists
  Verify: test -f tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline-relates-to.sh
- [ ] Test file is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline-relates-to.sh
- [ ] Tests check for relates_to suggestion section in pipeline
  Verify: grep -q "relates_to" tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline-relates-to.sh
- [ ] Tests check for user approval gate
  Verify: grep -q "approval\|user.*confirm\|AskUser" tests/skills/test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline-relates-to.sh
